name: pyserde

metadata.name: pyserde
metadata.summary: Yet another serialization library on top of dataclasses

documentation:
pyserde

Yet another serialization library on top of dataclasses, inspired by serde-rs.

Guide🇬🇧 | ガイド🇯🇵 | API Reference | Examples

## Overview `pyserde` is a simple yet powerful serialization library on top of [dataclasses](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html). It allows you to convert Python objects to and from JSON, YAML, and other formats easily and efficiently. Declare your class with `@serde` decorator and annotate fields using [PEP484](https://peps.python.org/pep-0484/) as below. ```python @serde class Foo: i: int s: str f: float b: bool ``` You can serialize `Foo` object into JSON. ```python >>> to_json(Foo(i=10, s='foo', f=100.0, b=True)) '{"i":10,"s":"foo","f":100.0,"b":true}' ``` You can deserialize JSON into `Foo` object. ```python >>> from_json(Foo, '{"i": 10, "s": "foo", "f": 100.0, "b": true}') Foo(i=10, s='foo', f=100.0, b=True) ``` That's it! If you're interested in pyserde, please check our documentation! Happy coding with pyserde! 🚀 * [Getting started](https://yukinarit.github.io/pyserde/guide/en/getting-started.html) * [API Reference](https://yukinarit.github.io/pyserde/api/serde.html) * [Examples](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/tree/main/examples) ## Features - Supported data formats - dict - tuple - JSON - Yaml - Toml - MsgPack - Pickle - Supported types - Primitives (`int`, `float`, `str`, `bool`) - Containers - `list`, `set`, `tuple`, `dict` - [`frozenset`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html#frozenset), [`defaultdict`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/collections.html#collections.defaultdict) - [`typing.Optional`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Optional) - [`typing.Union`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Union) - User defined class with [`@dataclass`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html) - [`typing.NewType`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#newtype) for primitive types - [`typing.Any`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#the-any-type) - [`typing.Literal`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Literal) - [`typing.Generic`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#user-defined-generic-types) - [`typing.ClassVar`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.ClassVar) - [`dataclasses.InitVar`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/dataclasses.html#init-only-variables) - [`Enum`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/enum.html#enum.Enum) and [`IntEnum`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/enum.html#enum.IntEnum) - Standard library - [`pathlib.Path`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/pathlib.html) - [`decimal.Decimal`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/decimal.html) - [`uuid.UUID`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/uuid.html) - [`datetime.date`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#date-objects), [`datetime.time`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#time-objects), [`datetime.datetime`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#datetime-objects) - [`ipaddress`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/ipaddress.html) - PyPI library - [`numpy`](https://github.com/numpy/numpy) types - [`SQLAlchemy`](https://github.com/sqlalchemy/sqlalchemy) Declarative Dataclass Mapping (experimental) - [Class Attributes](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/class-attributes.md) - [Field Attributes](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/field-attributes.md) - [Decorators](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/decorators.md) - [Type Check](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/type-check.md) - [Union Representation](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/union.md) - [Forward reference](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/decorators.md#how-can-i-use-forward-references) - [PEP563 Postponed Evaluation of Annotations](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/decorators.md#pep563-postponed-evaluation-of-annotations) - [PEP585 Type Hinting Generics In Standard Collections](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/getting-started.md#pep585-and-pep604) - [PEP604 Allow writing union types as X | Y](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/getting-started.md#pep585-and-pep604) - [PEP681 Data Class Transform](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/decorators.md#serde) - [Case Conversion](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/class-attributes.md#rename_all) - [Rename](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/field-attributes.md#rename) - [Alias](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/field-attributes.md#alias) - Skip (de)serialization ([skip](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/field-attributes.md#skip), [skip_if](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/field-attributes.md#skip_if), [skip_if_false](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/field-attributes.md#skip_if_false), [skip_if_default](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/field-attributes.md#skip_if_default)) - [Custom field (de)serializer](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/field-attributes.md#serializerdeserializer) - [Custom class (de)serializer](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/class-attributes.md#class_serializer--class_deserializer) - [Custom global (de)serializer](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/extension.md#custom-global-deserializer) - [Flatten](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde/blob/main/docs/en/field-attributes.md#flatten) ## Extensions * [pyserde-timedelta](https://github.com/yukinarit/pyserde-timedelta): (de)serializing datetime.timedelta in ISO 8601 duration format. ## Contributors ✨ Thanks goes to these wonderful people ([emoji key](https://allcontributors.org/docs/en/emoji-key)):

modules.name: serde.toml
documentation:
Serialize and Deserialize in TOML format. This module depends on
[tomli](https://github.com/hukkin/tomli) (for python<=3.10) and
[tomli-w](https://github.com/hukkin/tomli-w) packages.

functions.name: serde.toml.to_toml
documentation:
Serialize the object into TOML.

You can pass any serializable `obj`. If you supply keyword arguments other than `se`,
they will be passed in `toml_w.dumps` function.

* `skip_none`: When set to True, any field in the class with a None value is excluded from the
serialized output. Defaults to True.

If you want to use the other toml package, you can subclass `TomlSerializer` and implement
your own logic.

functions.name: serde.toml.from_toml
documentation: For Union, Optional etc.

functions.name: serde.toml.from_toml
documentation:
Deserialize from TOML into the object.

`c` is a class obejct and `s` is TOML string. If you supply keyword arguments other than `de`,
they will be passed in `toml.loads` function.

If you want to use the other toml package, you can subclass `TomlDeserializer` and implement
your own logic.

modules.name: serde.pickle
documentation: Serialize and Deserialize in Pickle format.

functions.name: serde.pickle.from_pickle
documentation: For Union, Optional etc.

modules.name: serde.json
documentation: Serialize and Deserialize in JSON format.

functions.name: serde.json.to_json
documentation:
Serialize the object into JSON str. [orjson](https://github.com/ijl/orjson)
will be used if installed.

You can pass any serializable `obj`. If you supply other keyword arguments,
they will be passed in `dumps` function.
By default, numpy objects are serialized, this behaviour can be customized with the `option`
argument with [orjson](https://github.com/ijl/orjson#numpy), or the `default` argument with
Python standard json library.

* `skip_none`: When set to True, any field in the class with a None value is excluded from the
serialized output. Defaults to False.

If you want to use another json package, you can subclass `JsonSerializer` and implement
your own logic.

functions.name: serde.json.from_json
documentation: For Union, Optional etc.

functions.name: serde.json.from_json
documentation:
Deserialize from JSON into the object. [orjson](https://github.com/ijl/orjson) will be used
if installed.

`c` is a class obejct and `s` is JSON bytes or str. If you supply other keyword arguments,
they will be passed in `loads` function.

If you want to use another json package, you can subclass `JsonDeserializer` and implement
your own logic.

modules.name: serde.msgpack
documentation:
Serialize and Deserialize in MsgPack format. This module depends on
[msgpack](https://pypi.org/project/msgpack/) package.

functions.name: serde.msgpack.to_msgpack
documentation:
Serialize the object into MsgPack.

You can pass any serializable `obj`. If `ext_dict` option is specified, `obj` is encoded
as a `msgpack.ExtType` If you supply other keyword arguments, they will be passed in
`msgpack.packb` function.

* `named`: If `named` is True, field names are preserved, namely the object is encoded as `dict`
then serialized into MsgPack.  If `named` is False, the object is encoded as `tuple` then
serialized into MsgPack. `named=False` will produces compact binary.

* `skip_none`: When set to True, any field in the class with a None value is excluded from the
serialized output. Defaults to False.

If you want to use the other msgpack package, you can subclass `MsgPackSerializer` and
implement your own logic.

functions.name: serde.msgpack.from_msgpack
documentation:
Deserialize from MsgPack into the object.

`c` is a class obejct and `s` is MsgPack binary. If `ext_dict` option is specified,
`c` is ignored and type is inferred from `msgpack.ExtType` If you supply other keyword
arguments, they will be passed in `msgpack.unpackb` function.

If you want to use the other msgpack package, you can subclass `MsgPackDeserializer`
and implement your own logic.

modules.name: serde
documentation:
```
.. include:: ../README.md
```

\## Modules

The following modules provide the core functionalities of `pyserde`.
\* `serde.se`: All about serialization.
\* `serde.de`: All about deserialization.
\* `serde.core`: Core module used by `serde.se` and `serde.de` modules.
\* `serde.compat`: Compatibility layer which handles mostly differences of `typing` module between
python versions.

The following modules provide pyserde's (de)serialize APIs.
\* `serde.json`: Serialize and Deserialize in JSON.
\* `serde.msgpack`: Serialize and Deserialize in MsgPack.
\* `serde.yaml`: Serialize and Deserialize in YAML.
\* `serde.toml`: Serialize and Deserialize in TOML.
\* `serde.pickle`: Serialize and Deserialize in Pickle.

Other modules
\* `serde.inspect`: Prints generated code by pyserde.

functions.name: serde.serde
documentation: serde decorator. Keyword arguments are passed in `serialize` and `deserialize`.

modules.name: serde.inspect
documentation:
pyserde inspection tool.

#### Usage

```
$ python -m serde.inspect <PATH> <NAME>

 PATH  Python script path.
 NAME  Pyserde class name.
```

functions.name: serde.inspect.inspect
documentation: Inspect a pyserde class.

functions.name: serde.inspect.main
documentation: Main entrypoint of `serde.inspect`.

modules.name: serde.se
documentation:
This module provides `serialize`, `is_serializable` `to_dict`, `to_tuple` and classes
and functions associated with serialization.

functions.name: serde.se.default_serializer
documentation:
Marker function to tell serde to use the default serializer. It's used when custom serializer
is specified at the class but you want to override a field with the default serializer.

functions.name: serde.se._make_serialize
documentation: Create a serializable class programatically.

functions.name: serde.se.serialize
documentation:
A dataclass with this decorator is serializable into any of the data formats
supported by pyserde.

```pycon
>>> from datetime import datetime
>>> from serde import serialize
>>> from serde.json import to_json
>>>
>>> @serialize
... class Foo:
...     i: int
...     s: str
...     f: float
...     b: bool
>>>
>>> to_json(Foo(i=10, s='foo', f=100.0, b=True))
'{"i":10,"s":"foo","f":100.0,"b":true}'
```

functions.name: serde.se.is_serializable
documentation:
Test if an instance or class is serializable.

```pycon
>>> @serialize
... class Foo:
...     pass
```

Testing `Foo` class object returns `True`.
\>>> is_serializable(Foo)
True

Testing `Foo` object also returns `True`.
\>>> is_serializable(Foo())
True

functions.name: serde.se.astuple
documentation: Serialize object into tuple.

functions.name: serde.se.to_tuple
documentation:
Serialize object into tuple.

```pycon
>>> @serialize
... class Foo:
...     i: int
...     s: str = 'foo'
...     f: float = 100.0
...     b: bool = True
>>>
>>> to_tuple(Foo(i=10))
(10, 'foo', 100.0, True)
```

You can pass any type supported by pyserde. For example,

```pycon
>>> lst = [Foo(i=10), Foo(i=20)]
>>> to_tuple(lst)
[(10, 'foo', 100.0, True), (20, 'foo', 100.0, True)]
```

functions.name: serde.se.asdict
documentation: Serialize object into dictionary.

functions.name: serde.se.to_dict
documentation:
Serialize object into python dictionary. This function ensures that the dataclass's fields are
accurately represented as key-value pairs in the resulting dictionary.

- `o`: Any pyserde object that you want to convert to `dict`
- `c`: Optional class argument
- `reuse_instances`: pyserde will pass instances (e.g. Path, datetime) directly to serializer

instead of converting them to serializable representation e.g. string. This behaviour allows
to delegate serializtation to underlying data format packages e.g. `pyyaml` and potentially
improve performance.
\* `convert_sets`: This option controls how sets are handled during serialization and
deserialization. When `convert_sets` is set to True, pyserde will convert sets to lists during
serialization and back to sets during deserialization. This is useful for data formats that
do not natively support sets.
\* `skip_none`: When set to True, any field in the class with a None value is excluded from the
serialized output. Defaults to False.

```pycon
>>> from serde import serde
>>> @serde
... class Foo:
...     i: int
...     s: str = 'foo'
...     f: float = 100.0
...     b: bool = True
>>>
>>> to_dict(Foo(i=10))
{'i': 10, 's': 'foo', 'f': 100.0, 'b': True}
```

You can serialize not only pyserde objects but also objects of any supported types. For example,
the following example serializes list of pyserde objects into dict.

```pycon
>>> lst = [Foo(i=10), Foo(i=20)]
>>> to_dict(lst)
[{'i': 10, 's': 'foo', 'f': 100.0, 'b': True}, {'i': 20, 's': 'foo', 'f': 100.0, 'b': True}]
```

functions.name: serde.se.sefields
documentation: Iterate fields for serialization.

functions.name: serde.se.render_union_func
documentation: Render function that serializes a field with union type.

functions.name: serde.se.enum_value
documentation: Helper function to get value from enum or enum compatible value.

variables.name: serde.se.SerializeFunc
documentation: Interface of Custom serialize function.

classes.name: serde.se.Serializer
documentation:
`Serializer` base class. Subclass this to customize serialize behaviour.

See `serde.json.JsonSerializer` and `serde.msgpack.MsgPackSerializer` for example usage.

classes.name: serde.se.SeField

methods.name: serde.se.SeField.varname
documentation: Get variable name in the generated code e.g. obj.a.b
Field class for serialization.

classes.name: serde.se.LRenderer

methods.name: serde.se.LRenderer.render
documentation: Render lvalue

methods.name: serde.se.LRenderer.flatten
documentation: Render field with flatten attribute.
Render lvalue for various types.

classes.name: serde.se.Renderer

methods.name: serde.se.Renderer.render
documentation: Render rvalue

methods.name: serde.se.Renderer.custom_field_serializer
documentation: Render rvalue for the field with custom serializer.

methods.name: serde.se.Renderer.dataclass
documentation: Render rvalue for dataclass.

methods.name: serde.se.Renderer.opt
documentation: Render rvalue for optional.

methods.name: serde.se.Renderer.list
documentation: Render rvalue for list.

methods.name: serde.se.Renderer.set
documentation: Render rvalue for set.

methods.name: serde.se.Renderer.tuple
documentation: Render rvalue for tuple.

methods.name: serde.se.Renderer.dict
documentation: Render rvalue for dict.

methods.name: serde.se.Renderer.primitive
documentation: Render rvalue for primitives.

class_variables.name: serde.se.Renderer.suppress_coerce
documentation: Suppress type coercing because generated union serializer has its own type checking
Render rvalue for code generation.

functions.name: serde.numpy.is_bare_numpy_array
documentation:
Test if the type is `np.ndarray` or `npt.NDArray` without type args.

```pycon
>>> import numpy as np
>>> import numpy.typing as npt
>>> is_bare_numpy_array(npt.NDArray[np.int64])
False
>>> is_bare_numpy_array(npt.NDArray)
True
>>> is_bare_numpy_array(np.ndarray)
True
```

modules.name: serde.de
documentation:
This module provides `deserialize`, `is_deserializable` `from_dict`, `from_tuple` and classes
and functions associated with deserialization.

functions.name: serde.de.serde_legacy_custom_class_deserializer
documentation:
Handle custom deserialization. Use default deserialization logic if it receives `SerdeSkip`
exception.

```
param cls

Type of the field.

param datavar

The whole variable to deserialize from. e.g. "data"

param value

The value for the field. e.g. "data['i']"

param custom

Custom deserialize function.

param default

Default deserialize function.
```

functions.name: serde.de.default_deserializer
documentation:
Marker function to tell serde to use the default deserializer. It's used when custom
deserializer is specified at the class but you want to override a field with the default
deserializer.

functions.name: serde.de._make_deserialize
documentation: Create a deserializable class programatically.

functions.name: serde.de.deserialize
documentation:
A dataclass with this decorator is deserializable from any of the data formats supported
by pyserde.

```pycon
>>> from serde import deserialize
>>> from serde.json import from_json
>>>
>>> @deserialize
... class Foo:
...     i: int
...     s: str
...     f: float
...     b: bool
>>>
>>> from_json(Foo, '{"i": 10, "s": "foo", "f": 100.0, "b": true}')
Foo(i=10, s='foo', f=100.0, b=True)
```

functions.name: serde.de.is_deserializable
documentation:
Test if an instance or class is deserializable.

```pycon
>>> @deserialize
... class Foo:
...     pass
>>>
>>> is_deserializable(Foo)
True
```

functions.name: serde.de.from_obj
documentation:
Deserialize from an object into an instance of the type specified as arg `c`.
`c` can be either primitive type, `list`, `tuple`, `dict` or `deserialize` class.

functions.name: serde.de.from_dict
documentation:
Deserialize dictionary into object.

```pycon
>>> @deserialize
... class Foo:
...     i: int
...     s: str = 'foo'
...     f: float = 100.0
...     b: bool = True
>>>
>>> from_dict(Foo, {'i': 10, 's': 'foo', 'f': 100.0, 'b': True})
Foo(i=10, s='foo', f=100.0, b=True)
```

You can pass any type supported by pyserde. For example,

```pycon
>>> lst = [{'i': 10, 's': 'foo', 'f': 100.0, 'b': True},
...        {'i': 20, 's': 'foo', 'f': 100.0, 'b': True}]
>>> from_dict(list[Foo], lst)
[Foo(i=10, s='foo', f=100.0, b=True), Foo(i=20, s='foo', f=100.0, b=True)]
```

functions.name: serde.de.from_tuple
documentation:
Deserialize tuple into object.

```pycon
>>> @deserialize
... class Foo:
...     i: int
...     s: str = 'foo'
...     f: float = 100.0
...     b: bool = True
>>>
>>> from_tuple(Foo, (10, 'foo', 100.0, True))
Foo(i=10, s='foo', f=100.0, b=True)
```

You can pass any type supported by pyserde. For example,

```pycon
>>> lst = [(10, 'foo', 100.0, True), (20, 'foo', 100.0, True)]
>>> from_tuple(list[Foo], lst)
[Foo(i=10, s='foo', f=100.0, b=True), Foo(i=20, s='foo', f=100.0, b=True)]
```

variables.name: serde.de.DeserializeFunc
documentation: Interface of Custom deserialize function.

classes.name: serde.de.Deserializer

methods.name: serde.de.Deserializer.deserialize
documentation:
deserialize `data` into an object typically `dict`, `list` or `tuple`.

For example, `serde.json.JsonDeserializer` takes json string and deserialize
into an object. `serde.msgpack.MsgPackDeserializer` takes msgpack bytes and
deserialize into an object.

`Deserializer` base class. Subclass this to customize deserialize behaviour.

See `serde.json.JsonDeserializer` and `serde.msgpack.MsgPackDeserializer` for example usage.

classes.name: serde.de.DeField

methods.name: serde.de.DeField.__getitem__
documentation:
Get inner `Field` from current `Field`.

`InnerField` is returned if self is of any standard collection e.g. list.
`DeField` is returned if self is Optional.

methods.name: serde.de.DeField.key_field
documentation: Get inner key field for dict like class.

methods.name: serde.de.DeField.value_field
documentation: Get inner value field for dict like class.

methods.name: serde.de.DeField.data
documentation:
Renders the variable name that possesses the data.

e.g. tuple
    * datavar property returns "data"
    * data property returns "data[0]".
e.g. Optional
    * datavar property returns "data"
    * data property returns "data.get("field_name")".
For other types
    * datavar property returns "data"
    * data property returns "data["field_name"]".

class_variables.name: serde.de.DeField.datavar
documentation: Name of variable which is passed in the deserialize API

class_variables.name: serde.de.DeField.index
documentation: Field index

class_variables.name: serde.de.DeField.iterbased
documentation: Iterater based deserializer or not
Represents a field of dataclass.

classes.name: serde.de.InnerField
documentation:
Field of Inner type. The purpose of this class is to override "data" method
for inner type codegen.

e.g.
  T of list[T]
  V of dict[K, V]
  T of Optional[T]

methods.name: serde.de.Renderer.custom_field_deserializer

methods.name: serde.de.Renderer.custom_class_deserializer
documentation: Render custom class deserializer.

methods.name: serde.de.Renderer.opt
documentation: Render rvalue for Optional.

methods.name: serde.de.Renderer.list
documentation: Render rvalue for list.

methods.name: serde.de.Renderer.set
documentation: Render rvalue for set.

methods.name: serde.de.Renderer.tuple
documentation: Render rvalue for tuple.

methods.name: serde.de.Renderer.dict
documentation: Render rvalue for dict.

methods.name: serde.de.Renderer.primitive
documentation:
Render rvalue for primitives.

* `suppress_coerce`: Overrides "suppress_coerce" in the Renderer's field

methods.name: serde.de.Renderer.default
documentation: Renders supplying default value during deserialization.

class_variables.name: serde.de.Renderer.suppress_coerce
documentation: Disable type coercing in codegen
Render rvalue for code generation.

modules.name: serde.yaml
documentation:
Serialize and Deserialize in YAML format. This module depends on
[pyyaml](https://pypi.org/project/PyYAML/) package.

functions.name: serde.yaml.to_yaml
documentation:
Serialize the object into YAML.

You can pass any serializable `obj`. If you supply keyword arguments other than `se`,
they will be passed in `yaml.safe_dump` function.

* `skip_none`: When set to True, any field in the class with a None value is excluded from the
serialized output. Defaults to False.

If you want to use the other yaml package, you can subclass `YamlSerializer` and implement
your own logic.

functions.name: serde.yaml.from_yaml
documentation: For Union, Optional etc.

functions.name: serde.yaml.from_yaml
documentation:
`c` is a class obejct and `s` is YAML string. If you supply keyword arguments other than `de`,
they will be passed in `yaml.safe_load` function.

If you want to use the other yaml package, you can subclass `YamlDeserializer` and implement
your own logic.

modules.name: serde.core
documentation: pyserde core module.

functions.name: serde.core.gen
documentation: A wrapper of builtin `exec` function.

functions.name: serde.core.add_func
documentation:
Generate a function and add it to a Scope's `funcs` dictionary.

* `serde_scope`: the Scope instance to modify
* `func_name`: the name of the function
* `func_code`: the source code of the function
* `globals`: global variables that should be accessible to the generated function

functions.name: serde.core.is_instance
documentation:
pyserde's own `isinstance` helper. It accepts subscripted generics e.g. `list[int]` and
deeply check object against declared type.

functions.name: serde.core.field
documentation: Declare a field with parameters.

functions.name: serde.core.fields
documentation: Iterate fields of the dataclass and returns `serde.core.Field`.

functions.name: serde.core.conv
documentation: Convert field name.

functions.name: serde.core.union_func_name
documentation:
Generate a function name that contains all union types

- `prefix` prefix to distinguish between serializing and deserializing
- `union_args`: type arguments of a Union

```pycon
>>> from ipaddress import IPv4Address
>>> union_func_name("union_se", [int, list[str], IPv4Address])
'union_se_int_list_str__IPv4Address'
```

functions.name: serde.core.literal_func_name
documentation:
Generate a function name with all literals and corresponding types specified with Literal[...]

- `literal_args`: arguments of a Literal

```pycon
>>> literal_func_name(["r", "w", "a", "x", "r+", "w+", "a+", "x+"])
'literal_de_r_str_w_str_a_str_x_str_r__str_w__str_a__str_x__str'
```

functions.name: serde.core.should_impl_dataclass
documentation:
Test if class doesn't have @dataclass.

`dataclasses.is_dataclass` returns True even Derived class doesn't actually @dataclass.
\>>> @dataclasses.dataclass
... class Base:
... a: int
\>>> class Derived(Base):
... b: int
\>>> dataclasses.is_dataclass(Derived)
True

This function tells the class actually have @dataclass or not.
\>>> should_impl_dataclass(Base)
False
\>>> should_impl_dataclass(Derived)
True

functions.name: serde.core.has_default
documentation:
Test if the field has default value.

```pycon
>>> @dataclasses.dataclass
... class C:
...     a: int
...     d: int = 10
>>> has_default(dataclasses.fields(C)[0])
False
>>> has_default(dataclasses.fields(C)[1])
True
```

functions.name: serde.core.has_default_factory
documentation:
Test if the field has default factory.

```pycon
>>> @dataclasses.dataclass
... class C:
...     a: int
...     d: dict = dataclasses.field(default_factory=dict)
>>> has_default_factory(dataclasses.fields(C)[0])
False
>>> has_default_factory(dataclasses.fields(C)[1])
True
```

functions.name: serde.core.add_serializer
documentation: Register custom global serializer.

functions.name: serde.core.add_deserializer
documentation: Register custom global deserializer.

variables.name: serde.core.CACHE
documentation: Global cache variable for non-dataclass classes

classes.name: serde.core.Cache

methods.name: serde.core.Cache._get_class
documentation:
Get a wrapper class from the the cache. If not found, it will generate
the class and store it in the cache.

methods.name: serde.core.Cache._generate_class
documentation:
Generate a wrapper dataclass then make the it (de)serializable using
@serde decorator.

methods.name: serde.core.Cache.serialize
documentation: Serialize the specified type of object into dict or tuple.

methods.name: serde.core.Cache.deserialize
documentation: Deserialize from dict or tuple into the specified type.

methods.name: serde.core.Cache._get_union_class
documentation:
Get a wrapper class from the the cache. If not found, it will generate
the class and store it in the cache.

methods.name: serde.core.Cache._generate_union_class
documentation:
Generate a wrapper dataclass then make the it (de)serializable using
@serde decorator.

methods.name: serde.core.Cache.serialize_union
documentation: Serialize the specified Union into dict or tuple.

methods.name: serde.core.Cache.deserialize_union
documentation: Deserialize from dict or tuple into the specified Union.

Cache the generated code for non-dataclass classes.

for example, a type not bound in a dataclass is passed in from_json

```
from_json(Union[Foo, Bar], ...)
```

It creates the following wrapper dataclass on the fly,

```
@serde
@dataclass
class Union_Foo_bar:
    v: Union[Foo, Bar]
```

Then store this class in this cache. Whenever the same type is passed,
the class is retrieved from this cache. So the overhead of the codegen
should be only once.

classes.name: serde.core.Scope

class_variables.name: serde.core.Scope.cls
documentation:
The exact class this scope is for
(needed to distinguish scopes between inherited classes)

class_variables.name: serde.core.Scope.funcs
documentation: Generated serialize and deserialize functions

class_variables.name: serde.core.Scope.defaults
documentation: Default values of the dataclass fields (factories & normal values)

class_variables.name: serde.core.Scope.code
documentation: Generated source code (only filled when debug is True)

class_variables.name: serde.core.Scope.union_se_args
documentation: The union serializing functions need references to their types

class_variables.name: serde.core.Scope.reuse_instances_default
documentation: Default values for to_dict & from_dict arguments
Container to store types and functions used in code generation context.

classes.name: serde.core.Func

methods.name: serde.core.Func.name
documentation: Mangled function name

class_variables.name: serde.core.Func.inner
documentation: Function to wrap in

class_variables.name: serde.core.Func.mangeld
documentation: Mangled function name

Function wrapper that provides `mangled` optional field.

pyserde copies every function reference into global scope
for code generation. Mangling function names is needed in
order to avoid name conflict in the global scope when
multiple fields receives `skip_if` attribute.

classes.name: serde.core.FlattenOpts
documentation: Flatten options. Currently not used.

classes.name: serde.core.Field

methods.name: serde.core.Field.from_dataclass
documentation: Create `Field` object from `dataclasses.Field`.

methods.name: serde.core.Field.mangle
documentation: Get mangled name based on field name.

methods.name: serde.core.Field.conv_name
documentation:
Get an actual field name which `rename` and `rename_all` conversions
are made. Use `name` property to get a field name before conversion.

class_variables.name: serde.core.Field.type
documentation: Type of Field

class_variables.name: serde.core.Field.name
documentation: Name of Field

class_variables.name: serde.core.Field.default
documentation: Default value of Field

class_variables.name: serde.core.Field.default_factory
documentation: Default factory method of Field

class_variables.name: serde.core.Field.serializer
documentation: Custom field serializer.

class_variables.name: serde.core.Field.deserializer
documentation: Custom field deserializer.

Field class is similar to `dataclasses.Field`. It provides pyserde specific options.

`type`, `name`, `default` and `default_factory` are the same members as `dataclasses.Field`.

classes.name: serde.core.Tagging

methods.name: serde.core.Tagging.produce_unique_class_name
documentation:
Produce a unique class name for this tagging. The name is used for generated
wrapper dataclass and stored in `Cache`.

Controls how union is (de)serialized. This is the same concept as in
https://serde.rs/enum-representations.html

classes.name: serde.core.TypeCheck

class_variables.name: serde.core.TypeCheck.Kind.Disabled
documentation: No check performed

class_variables.name: serde.core.TypeCheck.Kind.Coerce
documentation: Value is coerced into the declared type

class_variables.name: serde.core.TypeCheck.Kind.Strict
documentation: Value are strictly checked against the declared type
Specify type check flavors.

classes.name: serde.core.ClassSerializer
documentation:
Interface for custom class serializer.

This protocol is intended to be used for custom class serializer.

```pycon
>>> from datetime import datetime
>>> from serde import serde
>>> from plum import dispatch
>>> class MySerializer(ClassSerializer):
...     @dispatch
...     def serialize(self, value: datetime) -> str:
...         return value.strftime("%d/%m/%y")
```

classes.name: serde.core.ClassDeserializer
documentation:
Interface for custom class deserializer.

This protocol is intended to be used for custom class deserializer.

```pycon
>>> from datetime import datetime
>>> from serde import serde
>>> from plum import dispatch
>>> class MyDeserializer(ClassDeserializer):
...     @dispatch
...     def deserialize(self, cls: type[datetime], value: Any) -> datetime:
...         return datetime.strptime(value, "%d/%m/%y")
```

modules.name: serde.compat
documentation: Compatibility layer which handles mostly differences of `typing` module between python versions.

functions.name: serde.compat.is_hashable
documentation: Test is an object hashable

functions.name: serde.compat.cache
documentation: Wrapper for `functools.cache` to avoid `Hashable` related type errors.

functions.name: serde.compat.get_origin
documentation: Provide `get_origin` that works in all python versions.

functions.name: serde.compat.get_args
documentation: Provide `get_args` that works in all python versions.

functions.name: serde.compat.typename
documentation:
```pycon
>>> from typing import Any
>>> typename(int)
'int'
>>> class Foo: pass
>>> typename(Foo)
'Foo'
>>> typename(list[Foo])
'list[Foo]'
>>> typename(dict[str, Foo])
'dict[str, Foo]'
>>> typename(tuple[int, str, Foo, list[int], dict[str, Foo]])
'tuple[int, str, Foo, list[int], dict[str, Foo]]'
>>> typename(Optional[list[Foo]])
'Optional[list[Foo]]'
>>> typename(Union[Optional[Foo], list[Foo], Union[str, int]])
'Union[Optional[Foo], list[Foo], str, int]'
>>> typename(set[Foo])
'set[Foo]'
>>> typename(Any)
'Any'
```

functions.name: serde.compat.type_args
documentation: Wrapper to suppress type error for accessing private members.

functions.name: serde.compat.iter_types
documentation:
Iterate field types recursively.

The correct return type is `Iterator[Union[Type, typing._specialform]],
but `typing._specialform` doesn't exist for python 3.6. Use `Any` instead.

functions.name: serde.compat.iter_unions
documentation: Iterate over all unions that are used in the dataclass

functions.name: serde.compat.iter_literals
documentation: Iterate over all literals that are used in the dataclass

functions.name: serde.compat.is_union
documentation:
Test if the type is `typing.Union`.

```pycon
>>> is_union(Union[int, str])
True
```

functions.name: serde.compat.is_opt
documentation:
Test if the type is `typing.Optional`.

```pycon
>>> is_opt(Optional[int])
True
>>> is_opt(Optional)
True
>>> is_opt(None.__class__)
False
```

functions.name: serde.compat.is_bare_opt
documentation:
Test if the type is `typing.Optional` without type args.
\>>> is_bare_opt(Optional[int])
False
\>>> is_bare_opt(Optional)
True
\>>> is_bare_opt(None.\_\_class\_\_)
False

functions.name: serde.compat.is_opt_dataclass
documentation:
Test if the type is optional dataclass.

```pycon
>>> is_opt_dataclass(Optional[int])
False
>>> @dataclasses.dataclass
... class Foo:
...     pass
>>> is_opt_dataclass(Foo)
False
>>> is_opt_dataclass(Optional[Foo])
False
```

functions.name: serde.compat.is_list
documentation:
Test if the type is `list`.

```pycon
>>> is_list(list[int])
True
>>> is_list(list)
True
```

functions.name: serde.compat.is_bare_list
documentation:
Test if the type is `list` without type args.

```pycon
>>> is_bare_list(list[int])
False
>>> is_bare_list(list)
True
```

functions.name: serde.compat.is_tuple
documentation: Test if the type is tuple.

functions.name: serde.compat.is_bare_tuple
documentation:
Test if the type is tuple without type args.

```pycon
>>> is_bare_tuple(tuple[int, str])
False
>>> is_bare_tuple(tuple)
True
```

functions.name: serde.compat.is_variable_tuple
documentation:
Test if the type is a variable length of tuple tuple[T, ...]\`.

```pycon
>>> is_variable_tuple(tuple[int, ...])
True
>>> is_variable_tuple(tuple[int, bool])
False
>>> is_variable_tuple(tuple[()])
False
```

functions.name: serde.compat.is_set
documentation:
Test if the type is `set` or `frozenset`.

```pycon
>>> is_set(set[int])
True
>>> is_set(set)
True
>>> is_set(frozenset[int])
True
```

functions.name: serde.compat.is_bare_set
documentation:
Test if the type is `set` without type args.

```pycon
>>> is_bare_set(set[int])
False
>>> is_bare_set(set)
True
```

functions.name: serde.compat.is_frozen_set
documentation:
Test if the type is `frozenset`.

```pycon
>>> is_frozen_set(frozenset[int])
True
>>> is_frozen_set(set)
False
```

functions.name: serde.compat.is_dict
documentation:
Test if the type is dict.

```pycon
>>> is_dict(dict[int, int])
True
>>> is_dict(dict)
True
>>> is_dict(defaultdict[int, int])
True
```

functions.name: serde.compat.is_bare_dict
documentation:
Test if the type is `dict` without type args.

```pycon
>>> is_bare_dict(dict[int, str])
False
>>> is_bare_dict(dict)
True
```

functions.name: serde.compat.is_default_dict
documentation:
Test if the type is `defaultdict`.

```pycon
>>> is_default_dict(defaultdict[int, int])
True
>>> is_default_dict(dict[int, int])
False
```

functions.name: serde.compat.is_none
documentation:
```pycon
>>> is_none(int)
False
>>> is_none(type(None))
True
>>> is_none(None)
False
```

functions.name: serde.compat.is_enum
documentation: Test if the type is `enum.Enum`.

functions.name: serde.compat.is_primitive_subclass
documentation:
Test if the type is a subclass of primitive type.

```pycon
>>> is_primitive_subclass(str)
False
>>> class Str(str):
...     pass
>>> is_primitive_subclass(Str)
True
```

functions.name: serde.compat.is_primitive
documentation:
Test if the type is primitive.

```pycon
>>> is_primitive(int)
True
>>> class CustomInt(int):
...     pass
>>> is_primitive(CustomInt)
True
```

functions.name: serde.compat.is_new_type_primitive
documentation: Test if the type is a NewType of primitives.

functions.name: serde.compat.is_generic
documentation:
Test if the type is derived from `typing.Generic`.

```pycon
>>> T = typing.TypeVar('T')
>>> class GenericFoo(typing.Generic[T]):
...     pass
>>> is_generic(GenericFoo[int])
True
>>> is_generic(GenericFoo)
False
```

functions.name: serde.compat.is_class_var
documentation:
Test if the type is `typing.ClassVar`.

```pycon
>>> is_class_var(ClassVar[int])
True
>>> is_class_var(ClassVar)
True
```

functions.name: serde.compat.is_literal
documentation:
Test if the type is derived from `typing.Literal`.

```pycon
>>> T = typing.TypeVar('T')
>>> class GenericFoo(typing.Generic[T]):
...     pass
>>> is_generic(GenericFoo[int])
True
>>> is_generic(GenericFoo)
False
```

functions.name: serde.compat.is_any
documentation: Test if the type is `typing.Any`.

functions.name: serde.compat.is_str_serializable
documentation: Test if the type is serializable to `str`.

functions.name: serde.compat.is_datetime
documentation: Test if the type is any of the datetime types..

functions.name: serde.compat.get_type_var_names
documentation:
Get type argument names of a generic class.

```pycon
>>> T = typing.TypeVar('T')
>>> class GenericFoo(typing.Generic[T]):
...     pass
>>> get_type_var_names(GenericFoo)
['T']
>>> get_type_var_names(int)
```

functions.name: serde.compat.find_generic_arg
documentation:
Find a type in generic parameters.

```pycon
>>> T = typing.TypeVar('T')
>>> U = typing.TypeVar('U')
>>> V = typing.TypeVar('V')
>>> class GenericFoo(typing.Generic[T, U]):
...     pass
>>> find_generic_arg(GenericFoo, T)
0
>>> find_generic_arg(GenericFoo, U)
1
>>> find_generic_arg(GenericFoo, V)
-1
```

functions.name: serde.compat.get_generic_arg
documentation:
Get generic type argument.

```pycon
>>> T = typing.TypeVar('T')
>>> U = typing.TypeVar('U')
>>> class GenericFoo(typing.Generic[T, U]):
...     pass
>>> get_generic_arg(GenericFoo[int, str], ['T', 'U'], ['T', 'U'], 0).__name__
'int'
>>> get_generic_arg(GenericFoo[int, str], ['T', 'U'], ['T', 'U'], 1).__name__
'str'
>>> get_generic_arg(GenericFoo[int, str], ['T', 'U'], ['U'], 0).__name__
'str'
```

variables.name: serde.compat.StrSerializableTypes
documentation: List of standard types (de)serializable to str

variables.name: serde.compat.DateTimeTypes
documentation: List of datetime types

classes.name: serde.compat._WithTagging

class_variables.name: serde.compat._WithTagging.inner
documentation: Union type .e.g Union[Foo,Bar] passed in from_obj.

class_variables.name: serde.compat._WithTagging.tagging

Intermediate data structure for (de)serializaing Union without dataclass.

classes.name: serde.compat.SerdeError
documentation: Serde error class.

classes.name: serde.compat.UserError

classes.name: serde.compat.SerdeSkip
